title: Bite or sting review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Supports documenting rapid-onset local reaction, redness, itch, type and severity, possible infection, systemic reaction, tick context, and worsening or systemic illness only when assessed.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact assessment section supports bite or sting type, timing, local redness, itch, inflammatory or allergic features, possible infection, systemic reaction, tick context, and severity without assigning a diagnosis.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports documenting rapid-onset local reaction, redness, itch, type and severity, possible infection, systemic reaction, tick context, and worsening or systemic illness only when assessed.

source.title: Insect bites and stings: antimicrobial prescribing — Recommendations

referral.text: Supports documenting infection development, rapid worsening, systemic illness, severe disproportionate pain, previous systemic reaction, immunocompromise, mouth, throat or eye location, exotic exposure, fever, persistent lesions, and travel context.
referral.rationale: The exact reassessment section supports clinician-assessed worsening, systemic illness, disproportionate pain, prior systemic reaction, immunocompromise, mouth, throat or eye location, fever, travel, and entered follow-up or referral status.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports documenting infection development, rapid worsening, systemic illness, severe disproportionate pain, previous systemic reaction, immunocompromise, mouth, throat or eye location, exotic exposure, fever, persistent lesions, and travel context.

source.title: Insect bites and stings: antimicrobial prescribing — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.8–1.1.11 — reassessment, referral, and specialist-advice qualifiers

source_limitations: Exact organism, exposure, travel, lesion findings, systemic reaction, infection, allergy, medicine, tetanus, procedure, referral, urgency, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
